nativeRange.habitat: Aquarium-trade hybrid cultivar, not found in the wild. Parent species (E. schlueteri 'Leopard' and E. barthii) originate from South American river and marsh systems.

imageCandidates.notes: Retailer product page showing classic green Ozelot leaves with dark leopard spotting; useful as a visual reference for reviewers to pick a Wikimedia or freely-licensed equivalent.
Retailer product photography of Ozelot Green; secondary visual reference.

summary: The Ozelot Sword is an aquarium-trade hybrid Echinodorus known for broad oval leaves speckled with dark leopard-like spots. It is generally considered one of the more forgiving large Echinodorus plants, suited to medium and larger planted tanks as a midground or background centerpiece.
careNotes: The Ozelot Sword is sold in two main color forms: 'Ozelot Green', which has green leaves with dark maroon-brown spots, and 'Ozelot Red', which produces reddish to bronze new leaves with brighter spots and wavy edges. Both forms share the same care requirements. Light demand is moderate, and stronger lighting tends to intensify the spot pattern and red coloration in susceptible specimens. This is a HEAVY ROOT FEEDER: a nutrient-rich substrate is strongly recommended, and root tabs should be placed near (not directly under) the crown and replenished regularly. The plant is particularly responsive to iron and trace micronutrients, which deepen leaf coloration and sharpen the leopard-spot pattern. Best positioned as a focal-point midground or background plant; in aquariums shorter than roughly 60 cm it will likely outgrow the layout. Newly planted specimens may shed their emersed-form (above-water-grown) leaves as they adjust to fully submerged growth — this is a normal transition.
breedingNotes: Propagates by adventitious plantlets ('daughter plants') that develop on long flower stalks emerging from the crown, plus occasional side runners. Once each plantlet has a small rosette and visible roots it can be cut from the stalk and planted in the substrate. Emersed plants may produce small white flowers; true seed production is uncommon in submerged aquarium setups. Mature crowns can also be carefully divided.

primary.name: Modern Aquarium — Ozelot Green
primary.notes: Retailer/care-guide page for Echinodorus sp. Ozelot ('Ozelot Green'). Provided family (Alismataceae), temperature 70–82 °F (21–28 °C), moderate light, CO2 optional but beneficial, moderate growth, midground-to-background placement, easy/beginner care, heavy root feeder, responsive to iron/micronutrients, propagation via runners/flower stalks with detachable plantlets, leopard-spot leaf description, and the existence of an 'Ozelot Red' sibling form.

additional.name: Buce Plant — Echinodorus Ozelot
additional.notes: Cross-checked hybrid parentage (E. schlueteri 'Leopard' × E. barthii), pH 6.0–8.0, size 8–18 in (~20–45 cm), moderate light, CO2 not necessary, fast growth, midground-to-background placement, heavy root feeder requiring nutrient-rich soil, adventitious-shoot propagation, leaf description (large ovoid leaves with hardy veins and dark spotting), and the existence of green and red color forms with wavy leaves and varied spot coloration in the red form.
